name: How to market your Dental Practice
description: Learn simple, effective strategies to market your dental practice and attract new patients. From boosting your online presence to engaging with the local community, these tips will help grow your practice and build lasting relationships.
main_title: How to market your Dental Practice
richtext:
Marketing your dental practice doesn’t have to be complicated – it’s all about connecting with the right people and showing them the value you bring. With the right strategies, you can build trust, boost your visibility, and turn new patients into loyal ones. From digital marketing to community involvement, there are plenty of ways to get your practice noticed. 

Let’s talk about some some easy, effective ways to market your dental practice and watch it grow.

TLDR: Marketing Tactics for your Dental Care Practice

  • Research where potential patients spend time (online, offline, community events) and focus on those platforms.
  • Claim and optimize your Google Business Profile and add your practice to relevant directories like Yelp, Healthgrades, and local listings.
  • Encourage patients to leave reviews on Google and other platforms to build trust.
  • Reach specific audiences with targeted Facebook ads based on demographics, interests, and behaviors.
  • Target potential patients nearby with location-based notifications and special offers.
  • Tools that can help

Let’s dive into it!

1. Find Out Where Patients Are Looking

Figure out where your potential patients spend their time, both online and offline. Do some quick research. Are they searching online directories, browsing social media, or relying on word-of-mouth recommendations? 

Maybe they’re attending local community events. Once you know where they are, you can focus your efforts there.

2. Guide Them to Discovery Channels

After identifying the key places patients look for dental services, make sure you’re visible there. Build a strong presence both online and offline. List your practice where it matters, and don’t shy away from offline strategies like community outreach or collaborating with local businesses.

3. Build Your Online Visibility

Your online presence is often the first impression patients have of your practice. Here’s how to make it count:

Claim and Optimize Local Listings

Start with your Google Business Profile. Make sure it’s complete with up-to-date contact info, office hours, and photos. This improves your chances of showing up when people search for dentists in your area.

Add Your Practice to Relevant Directories

Expand beyond Google. Add your practice to directories like Yelp, Healthgrades, and Bing Places. These platforms are where patients often go to find trusted dental professionals.

Double-Check Your Details Everywhere

Keep your name, address, and phone number (NAP) consistent across every listing. Even small mismatches can confuse patients or hurt your local search rankings.

Use the Right Keywords

Think about what patients are searching for phrases like “dentist near me,” “teeth whitening in [city],” or “family dentistry.” Add these keywords naturally in your descriptions and listings to increase your chances of showing up in searches.

Ask for Reviews

Reviews matter, a lot. They help build trust and attract new patients. After every appointment, encourage happy patients to leave reviews on Google or other platforms. Make it easy by providing a link or QR code.

Top 13 Marketing Tactics to market your Dental Business

Being out there is important, and what’s even more important is to make sure your dental practice is listed on the right platforms. This way, when people are searching for dental services, they find you. Focus on niche directories specific to the dental industry, along with the major platforms. Here are some to consider:

  • dentalcenter.com
  • freeclinicdirectory.org
  • clinicsearch.org
  • thedentisthub.org
  • optometrist-directory.info
  • dentistsdirectory.us
  • dentistintown.net
  • ehealthscores.com
  • dentpedia.ca

In addition to these, make sure your practice is on the usual suspects like Google My Business, Yelp, your local Chamber of Commerce, and Yellow Pages. When you create your listings, include these key details:

  • Practice name, address, and phone number (NAP)
  • Services offered
  • Business hours
  • Website
  • Email
  • Category
  • Brief description

Don’t forget about other details like parking availability, wheelchair access, or pet-friendly info if it applies.

2. Logo and Messaging

A unique logo can really set your dental practice apart. A solid logo and tagline are vital for building your brand. If you're not the design type, platforms like Upwork or Fiverr have designers who can help you create one. The logo should reflect your practice’s personality.

Alternatively, tools like Canva or Graphic Springs offer simple ways to design a logo on your own, which you can then refine with a professional. Either route, a good logo will make a lasting impact on your brand.

3. Use Click-to-Call Ads

Get people to call and book their appointments. Click-to-call ads on platforms like Google AdWords and Facebook make it easier for potential patients to reach you directly. These ads can easily be added to your existing campaigns and will boost the chances of getting more calls.

4. Run Facebook Ads

Facebook ads are a powerful way to reach your audience. With Facebook's targeting options, you can create ads that specifically target people by location, age, interests, and even behaviors.

To create an ad that resonates:

  • Write compelling copy
  • Use strong visuals (images or videos)
  • Include a clear call-to-action (CTA)

With Facebook’s targeting tools, you can reach the right people based on their:

  • Demographics (age, location, gender)
  • Interests (health, fitness, dental care)
  • Behavior (how they interact online)

The more precise you are in your targeting, the more likely your ad will convert viewers into patients.

5. Get Your Dental Practice Listed on Google My Business

Google My Business (GMB) is one of the best tools for boosting your online presence. It helps your practice show up in local searches and on Google Maps, especially in the top three results.

Key benefits:

  • Appear in local search and on Google Maps
  • Increase credibility with customer reviews
  • Post updates, promotions, and news directly on your listing
  • Track customer interactions like call clicks and direction requests

6. Build Your Business Reviews

Reviews are crucial for building trust and credibility. Not only do they help boost your search engine ranking, but they can also impact your chances of being found via voice search.

To get started with reviews:

  • Make sure you’re listed on key directories
  • Ask your patients for reviews
  • Provide simple instructions on how to leave a review
  • Use tools to automate your review management
  • Always respond to reviews (good or bad) within 24 hours

7. Optimize Your Website for Local Mobile and Voice Search

With more and more searches happening on mobile and through voice search, optimizing your website is critical.

Here’s how to do it:

  • Ensure your site is mobile-friendly (responsive design)
  • Improve loading speed (aim for under 3 seconds)
  • Make sure your site is easy to navigate on a phone
  • Use long-tail keywords that match voice search queries
  • Implement local schema markup so search engines understand your business better

By doing this, you’ll improve your chances of showing up in local mobile and voice search results.

8. Geofencing

Geofencing allows you to target people who are near your dental practice. By setting up a virtual boundary, you can send notifications or special offers to people who walk into the area around your clinic. This is a great way to attract walk-ins or remind potential patients to book an appointment.

9. Leverage Urgency and FOMO in Your Marketing

Urgency and FOMO (fear of missing out) are powerful psychological tools that can drive action. In the dental world, where health is a priority, creating urgency can encourage people to book appointments. You could offer time-sensitive promotions or discounts that expire in a few hours.

Highlighting the consequences of neglecting oral health can also generate a sense of urgency. Make patients aware of the problem and present your services as the solution. This strategy motivates potential patients to take action now rather than wait.

10. Add Your Business to Instagram

Instagram is a great platform to connect with your local community and show the human side of your practice. With over 96 million active users in the U.S. alone, it’s an effective tool for building your brand. Here’s how to leverage Instagram:

  • Target locals in your area with location tags.
  • Use relevant hashtags to boost visibility.
  • Post at peak times for better engagement.
  • Keep posts fun, interactive, and authentic.
  • Consider running Instagram ads to expand your reach.

11. Build Referral Programs

Referrals are a strong source of new patients in the healthcare industry. When satisfied patients recommend your practice to others, it helps you build a loyal client base. To set up a referral program:

  • Offer rewards or incentives for successful referrals.
  • Make the process simple for patients to refer others.
  • Keep it personal to make the experience more meaningful.

12. Sponsor a Charity or Community Event

Sponsoring local charity events or health camps is a great way to give back while increasing your practice's visibility. Whether it's a health fair, charity drive, or community event, being involved in local activities builds your brand reputation and strengthens connections within the community.

13. Create a Repository of Keywords for Ads and Content

A solid keyword strategy is essential for driving online traffic. By compiling a list of relevant, high-ranking keywords, you can optimize your website and ads to attract the right audience. Start by doing some basic Google searches or using tools like Ubersuggest to find industry-specific keywords. This will ensure your marketing efforts are targeted and improve your SEO results.

Top 4 tools that can help Dental Businesses

  1. Synup 

Synup is a platform that helps businesses manage their online presence with ease. It covers everything from keeping your business info accurate across listings to managing customer reviews and even social media. Perfect for agencies and resellers, Synup also offers managed services, making it a solid choice for those looking to streamline their efforts.

How to Get Started with Synup

Synup has affordable plans for every business. Signing up for Synup is simple. Sign up for a demo, and we’ll set up your account. You can connect your business locations to Synup and start managing all your listings in one place under the listings tab. 

  1. Dentrix 

Dentrix is a leading dental practice management software that helps streamline patient scheduling, billing, and charting. It’s an all-in-one tool that helps dental professionals save time and improve the overall patient experience. Dentrix also offers advanced features like customizable templates, patient management, and reporting capabilities.

  1. Solutionreach

Solutionreach is a powerful patient communication tool designed for dental practices. It allows you to automate appointment reminders, send marketing emails, and even manage patient reviews. With its easy-to-use platform, Solutionreach helps improve patient engagement, reduce no-shows, and build long-term relationships with your patients.

  1. CareStack

CareStack is a comprehensive dental practice management software designed to streamline all aspects of a dental office, from scheduling to patient communications. It includes integrated billing, scheduling, and a mobile-friendly patient portal. It’s also equipped with tools for managing team productivity and keeping everything organized.

‍

Summing it up

To wrap it up, marketing your dental practice doesn’t have to be a headache. It’s all about showing up where your potential patients are, whether that’s online or in the community, and giving them a reason to trust you. Focus on building a strong online presence, leveraging the power of referrals, and staying active in your local area. Be consistent, stay authentic, and don’t be afraid to try out new strategies. When you do, you’ll not only attract new patients but keep them coming back for years to come. It’s all about connecting, building trust, and delivering value. 

‍

FAQs

How do I advertise myself as a dentist?
Advertise yourself by building a strong online presence—optimize your Google Business Profile, encourage patient reviews, and run targeted ads on social media. Be sure to engage with your local community both online and offline to build trust.

How do dentists find clients?
Dentists find clients by focusing on local SEO, getting listed in relevant directories, and using word-of-mouth referrals. Hosting events or partnering with local businesses also helps increase visibility and attract new patients.

How do I create a dental marketing plan?
Start by identifying your target audience and where they spend their time, then outline strategies for online visibility, community involvement, and patient retention. Don’t forget to track performance and adjust as needed to keep growing.

How do dentists market themselves?
Dentists market themselves through online presence (website, social media, local SEO), patient referrals, and community engagement. Consistency is key—keeping your practice top-of-mind through regular updates and special offers can help build long-term relationships.
